legal_notice.title: VERITAS Dataset Export — Sovereign Trace Seal
content: This export was generated from VERITAS, the Community Damage Certification Platform.
**The seal proves:** This exact dataset, containing the reports described below,
existed at this exact moment, in this exact form, before any analysis, modification, or distribution.
- Number of reports in the dataset
- SHA-256 hash of the full dataset (JSON payload)
**Verification:** Anyone can recompute the SHA-256 hash of the exported file
and compare it to the hash sealed in the ledger. If they match, the dataset
has not been altered since export.
This seal is permanent and immutable. The export cannot be repudiated.
The ledger is the chain of custody.

fields.label: Number of Reports
fields.description: Total reports included in this export.

fields.label: Dataset SHA-256 Hash
fields.description: Cryptographic hash of the full exported dataset.

fields.label: Crisis Type
fields.description: Primary crisis type in this dataset.

fields.label: Geographic Bounds (Optional)
fields.description: Bounding box of reports in this export.
fields.placeholder: Min Lat: 40.5, Max Lat: 40.9, Min Lng: -74.3, Max Lng: -73.9

fields.label: Exported By
fields.description: Name or role of the person exporting the dataset.

fields.label: Declaration
options: I affirm this export was generated from VERITAS and accurately represents the dataset at the time of export.
I understand this seal is permanent and immutable.
I understand anyone can verify dataset integrity by recomputing the SHA-256 hash and comparing to the sealed value.

steps: Export sealed — Permanent entry in the Sovereign Trace ledger.
Integrity hash recorded — SHA-256 of full dataset.
Notification — Export ID and seal hash provided.
Verification — Recipients can verify dataset has not been altered.
after_submit.footer:
The dataset is sealed. The hash is the proof.

VERITAS | Sovereign Trace Protocol | Sheldon K. Salmon | aionsystem2026@gmail.com
